title: Uragoga morindiflora
taxonomy: Uragoga morindiflora is a plant species within the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, where its classification is as follows: the genus Uragoga belongs to the tribe Psychotrieae. The exact classification is further divided into the subfamily Ixoroideae. Uragoga morindiflora is closely related to other plants in the Rubiaceae family, such as those in the genus Psychotria. The classification of Uragoga morindiflora helps in understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
uses: Uragoga morindiflora is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa. It is valued for its potential to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections.
